name: Grilled Eggplant Salad with Onion & Cucumber
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the ends of the eggplants and cut into 3/4-inch thick slices.
2. Lightly brush eggplant slices with oil and place on a tray.
3. On a barbecue with lid, place grill 5-6 inches over a solid bed of hot coals (you can hold your hand at grill level only 2-3 seconds). When grill is hot, rub it lightly with a paper towel dipped in salad oil.
4. Lay eggplant slices close together on the grill. Cover barbecue, keeping drafts open, and cook until slices are well browned and soft when pressed, 15-20 minutes; turn as needed with a wide spatula. Return slices to the tray. If cooked ahead, cover and chill up to one day.
5. In a bowl, toss cucumber and onion slices in ice water. Quickly squeeze slices to bruise lightly, then cover and chill for 30 minutes to 1 hour; drain. In another bowl, mix cucumber, onion, vinegar, sugar, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. If made ahead, cover and chill up to 4 hours.
6. Coarsely chop eggplant and spoon onto a rimmed platter. Top with cucumber and onion mixture. Add salt to taste.
